values:
1. Improvement Contribution
A measurable improvement made beyond normal BAU.

2. Responsibilities
How consistently agreed monthly BAU responsibilities were owned and completed.

3. Expected Behaviours
How consistently the employee demonstrated the team's ways of working.

Each objective is scored 1–5 with evidence.

formula: =IFERROR(INDEX('Monthly Assessment'!A7:A36,MATCH(MAXIFS('Monthly Assessment'!H7:H36,'Monthly Assessment'!L7:L36,"Eligible"),'Monthly Assessment'!H7:H36,0)),"No eligible winner yet")

values: Completed 97% BAU responsibilities; closure record attached
Two peer shoutouts and no behaviour gaps

Responsibilities and Expected Behaviours must be rated using the measurable rubric and supported by evidence. Improvement score is the highest verified improvement level achieved that month. A winner must meet the overall minimum, every objective minimum, evidence requirement, and no-consecutive-winner rule.

preview: Completed 97% BAU responsibilities; closure record attached

values: Record the contribution, result and evidence. TL selects the verified 1–5 score using the rubric; the highest verified score for the month feeds the assessment.
Adopted by the whole team; measured 12% fewer corrections
Approval, accepted suggestion, or decision record.
Small change implemented in one task, process, or personal workflow.
Before/after example showing the change is in use.
Implemented and adopted by at least 2 colleagues, or used in one recurring team workflow.
Adoption proof, reusable file, or repeated-use record.
Adopted team-wide and demonstrates at least 10% improvement in time, errors, steps, or turnaround.
Baseline and after-result with calculation or system record.
Sustained for at least 2 months or adopted across sub-teams, with at least 20% measurable improvement or a recurring issue eliminated.
Trend, cross-team adoption, or documented elimination of the issue.
Less than 70% of agreed monthly BAU responsibilities completed, or repeated ownership gaps.
70%–84% of agreed monthly BAU responsibilities completed.
Completed items divided by agreed BAU items.
85%–94% completed; core responsibilities handled with only routine follow-up.
Completed items divided by agreed BAU items plus TL notes.
95%–99% completed; work independently owned and closed without escalation.
Completion record and evidence of closure/ownership.
100% completed, plus one documented example of proactive ownership, coverage, or issue prevention.
Completion record plus the proactive example.
Two or more documented behaviour gaps during the month.
One documented gap, or repeated reminders needed to meet team standards.
Consistently meets expected behaviours with no documented gap.
TL observation and monthly check-in record.
Meets level 3 and has at least 2 documented positive examples or peer acknowledgements.
Two dated examples, shoutouts, or feedback items.
Role-model behaviour: at least 3 positive examples and evidence that others adopted or benefited from the behaviour; no documented gaps.
Three examples plus adoption or team-impact evidence.
Scoring principle: rate the result demonstrated during the month—not effort or intention. If evidence does not support the selected level, use the highest lower level that is fully supported.

values: Evidence required for all three objectives

1. Staff or TL records improvement evidence in the Improvement Log.

2. TL verifies the improvement score against the 1–5 rubric.

3. TL completes Responsibilities and Expected Behaviours ratings in Monthly Assessment.

4. Evidence is mandatory for every rating.

5. Dashboard identifies the highest eligible overall score.

The three KPI objectives are separate from quarterly OKRs.
Weights are editable but must total 100%. Recommended default: equal weighting. Replace the placeholder names before using the tracker.
